title: Fargesia melanostachys
taxonomy: Fargesia melanostachys belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a species of bamboo, classified as a type of clumping bamboo due to its growth habit. The genus Fargesia is a group of non-fragrant, clumping bamboos native to China and Southeast Asia. Fargesia melanostachys is also known as the Yunnan clumping bamboo. It is a popular ornamental plant in gardens and landscapes due to its attractive foliage and compact growth.
uses: Fargesia melanostachys, also known as the black bamboo or Huang Sha Zhu, has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its purported health benefits. intro: Fargesia melanostachys, also known as the Tibetan bamboo or black-stemmed bamboo, is a species of bamboo native to the Tibetan Plateau.
